title: Level 899
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: ㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤ}} {{VoidedTheme}} {{Background|file=899bgimage2.jpg}} =
Level 899: '''"The Eternal Loop"'''
=
[[File:Meglogo-black-and-white.png|thumb]] '''M.E.G. Notice:'''
'''Level 899''' has been designated Impresario Class Priority. Due to its sanity-collapsing environment, only use machinery to document the level. Such means include, but are not limited to: drones, rovers, cameras, etc. Do not, under any circumstances, physically enter the level, for it will inevitably lead to either one meeting an untimely demise or a fate to eternally wander until their sanity has fully withered away.
[[File:CUD (FOR SD ARCHIVES ONLY).png|right|200px]]
'''Threat Index: Class Undetermined'''
'''》Crumbling Safety'''
'''》Critically Unstable'''
'''》Inconsistent Time Fluctuation'''
'''Level 899''' is the 900th level of the Backrooms and remains one of the most unexplored and undocumented numbered levels. It's well-known for its time-looping properties and is highly unadvised to enter.
'''Environment'''
{{Blurimage|file=899bgimage.png|caption=An image of Level 899. Note the poor quality of the camera used to take this photo.}} '''Level 899''' is a series of interconnected subway cars shrouded in darkness, save for the sparsely distributed functional fluorescent light panels placed throughout the cars. The color theme of the entirety of the level follows a monochrome scale of black and white, and all colorful objects brought onto the level will eventually fade to the same grayness. Foods and drinks that undergo this phenomenon will gradually lose their taste, eventually becoming a mush of blandness. This, unfortunately, also affects living things. However, this, as of currently, has shown no hazards being inflicted on the affected being. It is worth noting that the mentioned effect is permanent, and there is no way to restore the lost color.[[Object 1|'''Almond Water''']] will slightly restore some color, but this effect will quickly fade back into monochrome. The length of the main subway is presumed to be infinite, and it is located inside of a tunnel of the assumed same length. However its width measures only ten feet (approximately three meters) from one side to the other. The tunnel knows only two directions: forwards and backwards; its shape is completely straight, and no turns have been detected in the railings. The state in which the cars are in are severely variable with some being in perfect condition while others have been seen severely damaged and neglected. The cars of the subway vary in time design, too, with some being modern cars and others being simple boxcars. This has been theorized to be a result of the level's unusual time-altering property, which will be further elaborated upon in its own section. While no damaged cars have been reported to collapse, it's best to seek an exit immediately due to the inconsistent occurrences of the level's event. Furthermore, the cars' fluorescent lighting are more prone to serious explosions that may cause severe injuries.Blast injuries include (but are not limited to): internal organ injuries, lung and traumatic brain injury, extremity injuries, burns, hearing, and vision alternations. The tracks in which the subway lies on are comprised of metal and concrete, yet they have shown abnormally great durability. Stepping off of the subway is highly unadvised due to one's sanity critically plunging to irreversible limits to the point where any sanity-restoring methods will prove insufficient. Additionally, one's vision will almost completely blacken, sending a rush of fear through the wanderer. A faint mist drags lazily across the air, carrying a faint, toxic odor of metal. A haze of fog also clouds in the distance, but it easily parts upon contact. It is also worth noting that one's sanity slowly deteriorates in this level. Additionally, this fog appears to make thinking difficult, further promoting the consumption of [[Object 1|'''Almond Water''']].
'''The 899 Event'''
'''The 899 Event''' is a time fluctuating anomaly that occurs every so often. Unfortunately, there is no way to predict when the event will occur due to its inconsistent occurrences and durations. When '''The 899 Event''' begins, gravity will start to weaken, which can be proven by the random hovering of objects. One will experience a sudden drop in temperature, further amplifying the atmosphere. After a few seconds, time will suddenly reverse on itself, rewinding all action that had previously happened. The wanderer's vision will start to blur, and space will start to warp, making standing a difficult task. An incredibly severe quake will take place, which will severely damage the level's structure, mainly the tunnel. This level, however, does not repair itself like many other levels of the Backrooms. With this fact and the aforementioned quakes, it is predicted that this level is destined to collapse in on itself due to its failing stability for each time '''The 899 Event''' occurs. Thus, this level is best to be left as it is and wait for its inevitable end. We believe some sort of entity has caused this to happen, but this entity has never been seen or proven.
'''Entrances & Exits'''
Entrances *A black and white subway car inside the ground will lead here. Note that the environment immediately shifts to that of 899, making turning back impossible. *Boarding the metro at [[Level 898|'''Level 898''']] leads here. One reaches the metro station through a 30-meter-long cave. *Playing with a black and white clock may lead here. *Noclipping into a shadowed/dark area has an astronomically small change to lead here. *If one scales the [[Level 922|'''Eternal Staircase''']] long enough before jumping down in the middle, they will end up here. Exits *A gray door randomly placed in the tunnel will lead to [[Level 139|'''Level 139''']]. *Venturing far enough into a completely darkened area may lead to a variety of unilluminated levels, such as [[Level 6|'''Level 6''']], one of ditto's sublevels, et cetera. *Finding a car that is flooded with plastic balls and covering oneself with said balls will lead to [[Level 60|'''Level 60''']]. *A manhole in the floor of the subway leads to [[Level 800|'''Level 800''']]. *Finding a car that is made entirely out of stone, closing your eyes, and attempting to imagine something that does not exist will lead you to [[Level 900|'''Level 900''']], which is nonexistent.
'''Entities'''
{{Fullscreen|show={{Wavy|Level 899 is completely devoid of life.}}|hide='''Bow down to the Fallen God.'''|content= {{Collapse|num=8991|initial=[[File:899bgimage.png|center]]|final=|body= {{Collapse|num=8992|initial=[[File:899zoomup.png|400px|center]]|final=|body= {{Collapse|num=8993|initial=[[File:899zoomup2.png|400px|center]]|final=|body= {{Collapse|num=8994|initial=[[File:899zoomup3.png|400px|center]]|final=|body=
THE FALLEN GOD
'''''AT ALL COSTS'''''. Thus, an announcement has been made to all major organization to avoid any monochromatic subway level. Stories of ITS life do hit toward a method of eradicating, mainly involving the usag- '''WARNING: DATA CORRUPTED!''' {{Collapse|initial='''WARNING: DATABASE INFILTRATED!'''|final='''WARNING: DATABASE INFILTRATED!'''|num=5432532532534523452345|body=
You speak of words we do not understand. But we do understand that you are also against HIM. What form you elaborate upon is only his weakest. If HE truly unleashes HIS power upon our worlds, there is nothing that can stop HIM. But eons ago, when our world wasn't destroyed by HIM, we did indeed create a way to stop HIM. HIS weakness is being forgotten. To be lost to the cosmos. To be irrelevant. We advise you to never enter this "level", for it would mean he would be further forgotten. ''And when gods are forgotten for long enough, they fade away to nothing...'' }}}}}}}}}}
}}
'''Relevant Logs'''
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
Discovery Log
|final=
Discovery Log
|body= '''Agent Eris''': Guess we should've told 'em to look behind him, huh?
'''Agent Grayfield''': No shit, Sherlock. You saw the look on his face? Looked like he seen a ghost.
'''Agent Eris''': Oh well... Welp. Time to head back home. You ready?
'''Agent Grayfield''': Hell yeah. This level gives me them chills.
'''Agent Eris''': *giggling* ''Every'' level gives you the chills.
'''Agent Grayfield''': Shut.
'''Agent Eris''': *more giggling*
'''Agent Grayfield''': Let's just get outta here.
'''Agent Eris''': One step behind ya.
''''

'''Agent Grayfield''': This... doesn't look like Base.
'''Agent Eris''': Looks like we're in a new level. Get the camera.
'''Agent Grayfield''': Damn. It's all black and white. Almost as if somethin' sucked all the color away.
'''Agent Eris''': A lifeless, soulless place, devoid of hopes and spirits.
'''Agent Grayfield''': ...
'''Agent Eris''': ...
'''Agent Grayfield''': How long is this subway?
'''Agent Eris''': Knowing the Backrooms, it's probably infinite...
'''Agent Grayfield''': ...
'''Agent Eris''': How much you wanna bet that stepping down there is a death sentence?
'''Agent Grayfield''': *pulling out Almond Water* Let me see.
'''Agent Eris''': I thought levels gives you the chills. Now you're steppin' onto the tracks.
'''Agent Grayfield''': *sigh*
''''

'''Agent Grayfield''': *panicked* W-w-what the hell!? Shit's a fucking sanity p-plunger! Never g-goin' d-d-down there ag-gain!
'''Agent Eris''': Good thing you only put one foot down. If you'd jump, you'd be done for.
'''Agent Grayfield''': Note to self. Never go down onto the tracks.
'''Agent Eris''': Wanna take chances and try to noclip out?
''''

'''Agent Grayfield''': Wait what?
'''Agent Eris''': Weren't we just here a few seconds ago?
'''Agent Grayfield''': Woah! Almost put my foot back down there again.
'''Agent Eris''': Almost like time just reversed.
'''Agent Grayfield''': ... It probably did.
'''Agent Eris''': What?
'''Agent Grayfield''': Look at the clock up there. It was 5:14 when I almost stepped down. We spent two minutes talking, so it should've been 5:16. The clock still says 5:14!
'''Agent Eris''': Time fluctuation, huh? Interesting...
'''Agent Grayfield''': C'mon. Let's get outta here before time starts folding again.
'''Agent Eris''': Wait. Was the car behind us always collapsed?
'''Agent Grayfield''': ... Quakes.
'''Agent Eris''': Alright. Let's just get outta here.
'''Agent Grayfield''': Now who's the one getting chills?
''''
}}

Expedition Log
|final=
Expedition Log
|body= '''Agent A''': Y'all ready?
'''Agent B''': Ready as I'll ever be, man.
'''Agent C''': *jokingly* Sir, yes sir!
'''Agent A''': Alright. Reporting to Base. We have located a monochrome subway in [[Level 10]]. It's... sticking out of the ground. We are properly armed, and we will now enter the car.
''''

'''Agent B''': Y'all got your flashlights?
'''Agent A''': Mhmm.
'''Agent C''': Aye, I got it.
'''Agent B''': Camera?
'''Agent A''': Check!
'''Agent C''': Alright, guys. We've prepared for this for weeks.
'''Agent A''': Then why do I feel like I'm still gonna barf.
'''Agent B''': One has yet to experience stress when practicing. The true test comes when the real deal arrives.
'''Agent C''': I hear ya, Archimedes.
'''Agent B''': Be quiet.
''''

'''Agent B''': I'm curious 'bout something... Pass me the camera rover.
'''Agent A''': I gotchu.
'''Agent C''': What's on your mind?
'''Agent B''': What happens when we explore the tracks via machinery?
'''Agent A''': Hmm... I don't know. Go for it.
''''

'''Agent B''': Damn. Typical level.
'''Agent A''': *quietly* Natural causes... Got it.
'''Agent C''': Soooooooo... Continue through the cars?
''''

'''Agent A''': *shocked* Yo! What was that?
'''Agent C''': *seriously* Guns at the ready.
'''Agent A''': Triangle Formation A. C'mon.
'''Agent B''': Uhhh, is that clock moving backwards?
''''

'''Agent A''': They really weren't kidding about time fluctuation. How far back did we go?
'''Agent C''': Three minutes.
'''Agent B''': So does that affect how much time we have left before we head back?
'''Agent A''': Hopefully not. We got... five minutes left.
''''

'''Agent A''': WHAT THE FU-
